title: Pullup resistor?
author: JGeeforce
body:
Hi,

I see your website in renewed and I miss the part where you had the pictures about the pull up resistor. Over which wires do I have to place the pull up resistor?

Thanks!

title: KWH meter code update
author: Delusional112
body:
Hi,

I have a KWH meter connected to my electric car charger. It's an LEM022SJ with 400 pulses per KWH. I added the 400 to the options in the code.

I would like to read the KWH usage of the car charger. I have the ESP connected and online. But it is reading usage even if I'm not charging the car.

Danny

title: How to update manually via EspHome
author: zjean
body:
Hi,
I am using the s0tool solely as watermeter. It works well, I am very happy with it.
I made a few adjustments to the entity names when first flashing the s0tool via Esphome.
What are the steps of performing updates to my s0tool?

Which file of the repo should I use (and adjust it to my entity names)?
Thanks!

Below my current yaml, but I would like to incorporate the energy dashboard feature.

```
# Delen en bewerken mag maar laat deze eerste regels staan.
# Bron : https://www.huizebruin.nl
# Bron File : https://github.com/huizebruin/s0tool/tree/main/esphome
# © Huizebruin.nl
substitutions:
  vdate: "v22.08.15"
  device_description: watermeter (s0tool) gemaakt door huizebruin.nl versie ${vdate} .
  name: s0tool

esphome:
  name: ${name}
  platform: ESP8266
  board: d1_mini
  comment: '${device_description}'
  project:
    name: huizebruin.${name}
    version: "${vdate}"
  
dashboard_import:
  package_import_url: github://huizebruin/s0tool/esphome/watermeter.yaml

improv_serial:
  
wifi:
# secrets rechts boven binnen esphome (vanaf versie 2021.12.0 - 11th standaard in de secrets)
  networks:
    - ssid: !secret wifi_ssid
      password: !secret wifi_password
  fast_connect: true
  use_address: s0tool.local
  domain: ".local"
#------------------------##------------------------##------------------------##------------------------#
# ⬇ Hieronder niets veranderen tenzij je het overal goed aanpast ⬇ #
# ⬇ Schakel fallback-hotspot (captive portal) in voor het geval de wifi-verbinding mislukt ⬇ #
  ap:
    ssid: "${name}"

web_server:
  port: 80
  
captive_portal:
# ⬇ Enable logging ⬇ # 
logger:
  level: INFO
  
#  # ⬇ Enable Home Assistant API ⬇ # 
api:
  services:
    - service: meterstand_water
      variables:
        meter_value: int 
      then:
        - globals.set:
            id: totalWaterUsage
            value: !lambda "return ( meter_value ) ;"

globals:
  - id: totalWaterUsage
    type: int 
# ⬇ Bij het updaten onder de waarde, verander dit in no voor 1 run  ⬇ # 
    restore_value: no      
# ⬇ Bij het flashen,pas dit aan! ⬇ #        
    initial_value: '201' 

ota:
  safe_mode: true
  reboot_timeout: 10min
  num_attempts: 5

#  ⬇ Enable time component voor reset elke nacht ⬇ #
time:
  - platform: homeassistant
    id: time_homeassistant
    on_time_sync:
      - component.update: S0tool_uptime_timestamp

sensor:
#------------------------# Watermeter #------------------------#
# ⬇ watermeter pulsen ⬇ #
  - platform: pulse_counter
    pin: D2
    id: watermeter_pulse
    name: "watermeter pulse"
    state_class: measurement
    accuracy_decimals: 1

# ⬇ Totaal watermeter ⬇ #      
    total:
      id: sensor_pulse_meter_total
      name: "watermeter Totaal"
      icon: "mdi:cube-outline"
      state_class: "total_increasing"
      unit_of_measurement: m3
      accuracy_decimals: 3
      filters:
      - multiply: 0.001
      
# ⬇ Watermeter stand bij benadering ⬇ #
  - platform: template
    id: watermeter_total
    name: "watermeter stand"
    state_class: "total_increasing"
    icon: mdi:water
    unit_of_measurement: m3
    accuracy_decimals: 3
    lambda:       
      return id(sensor_pulse_meter_total).state + id(totalWaterUsage);
      
# ⬇ watermeter l/min ⬇ #
  - platform: template
    name: "Water verbruik Liter"
    id: watermeter_flow
    accuracy_decimals: 1
    unit_of_measurement: "l/min"
    icon: "mdi:water"
    lambda: return (id(watermeter_pulse).state);
#------------------------#  sensoren #------------------------#
  - platform: uptime
    id: S0tool_uptime

  - platform: template
    id: S0tool_uptime_timestamp
    name: "online sinds"
    device_class: "timestamp"
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    update_interval: never
    lambda: |-
      static float timestamp = (
        id(time_homeassistant).utcnow().timestamp - id(S0tool_uptime).state
      );
      return timestamp;  

  # WiFi signal
  - platform: wifi_signal
    name: "${name} - WiFi Signal"
    update_interval: 120s  
        
# ⬇ versie verbinding informatie sensoren voor in ha ⬇ #
  - platform: wifi_signal
    name: "${name} - WiFi Signal"
    update_interval: 120s
    
text_sensor:
  - platform: template
    name: "s0tool_versie"
    id: s0tool_versie
    icon: "mdi:label-outline"
    update_interval: 6h
    lambda: |-
      return {"${vdate}"};

  - platform: wifi_info
    ip_address:
      name: IP-adres
      id: s0tool_ip_adres
      icon: mdi:ip-network
    ssid:
      name: Netwerk
      id: s0tool_netwerk
      icon: mdi:access-point-network
    mac_address:
      name: Mac-adres
      id: s0tool_mac_adres
      icon: mdi:folder-key-network-outline

# ⬇ restart button voor de s0tool ⬇ #
button:  
  - platform: restart
    name: "Restart device"

```

title: [FR] Add list for water meters

First of I want to say great work with this project! I think that this will come in handy for many users that want to track water usage and so on.
I would like to see a list for water meters support this function. That will make it easier for others to see if they can use this for tracking water usage and then buy the needed components. title: Changes in release v23.02.29
author: Gyosa3
body:
Hi, I am running your box on my water meter and it's great :)

I was taken by surprise with the latest version, as I clicked "update" without checking the release note. I digested the change to "watermeter flow", that's a better name, but I seem to have missed what happened with the pulse sensor.

"wijziging van pulse_counter naar pulse_meter"

Am I right if I understand that you have completely removed it both in the web page and in the HA device/entities?  Just to be sure my setup is not going wrong...

Also, I received your setup with an initial meterstand of about 8m3, while my water meter was new, and I have not found a way to change the value to match my meter. In your docs it says that you can change the value in "Developer Tools" and set the state of that sensor, but that does not update the value in the device, probably because a sensor is read-only in HA?

Do you have any other method to write the value in the board itself? Shall I crate a new issue in Github for this maybe?

Thanks!!

Question how does the OTA update works  on the website of the sensor and which file do I need to upload.

title: kwh meter not working
author: AppleFlapp
body:
Hello,

Not sure if this is the right place for this but I give it a shot.

Yesterday I started using the pulse meter function of the S0 tool. But I have a few issues.
kWh sensor status is going nuts switching on and off and on and off and...

Another issue is that the entity Actuele Energie is not stable. Even when there is no load the value fluctuates. 
Pull up resistor also installed. This already did improve the flutcation of the Actuele Energie but it is still not ok.

And nothing changes when there is a load. Actuele Energie keeps doing what was described above.
Actuele meterstand stays on what it is set to.

When I disconnect the + line to the kWh meter pulse output the Actuele energie is counting up as well as the 
Actuele meterstad but still without load. 

I am using the Eastron SDM120D MID.

Programming the ESP was done via the website. 

I use the kWh meter to read out the use of an outlet socket.

Anyone who can help? I'm not an expert in this yet.

I see a few possible options at the moment.
1. Something in the code is wrong.
2. ESP board pin D5 is end of life.
3. The Eastron pulse output is not working.

title: Why pulse_meter is better of pulse_counter
author: array81
body:
I noticed that in "recent" times the sensor was changed from pulse_counter to pulse_meter in the water meter.
Why?
I use an NPN sensor on my water meter, the meter has a metal pointer. At each turn I count a pulse and therefore a liter. Wouldn't it be better to use the pulse_counter sensor?

title: Manual update
author: array81
body:
In the past (I think about 1 year ago) I used rge s0tool code to create my Water Meter.
At the time I found the code on github but I didn't realize that it was possible to install the code with the wizard so I created an esphome device and manually added the code extrapolating it from what I found on this repository. Now update the device using the official code but I have two problems. First: I would have to update the device via wireless because its access via USB and PC is complicated. Second: if possible I would like to keep the "history" on Home Assistant, i.e. I would like HA not to notice the change and continue to see it as the same device.
Is there a way to do what is indicated? If so, how should I proceed?

Here's my code:

```
substitutions:
  name: "water-meter"
  friendly_name: "Water Meter"
  device_description: Contatore acqua

esphome:
  name: ${name}

esp8266:
  board: d1_mini
  restore_from_flash : True

# Enable logging
logger:

# Enable Home Assistant API
api:
  encryption:
    key: "xxxxxx"
  services:
    - service: meterstand_water
      variables:
        meter_value: float
      then:
        - globals.set:
            id: initial_water_usage
            value: !lambda "return ( meter_value - (id(total_water_pulses)) * atof(id(select_pulse_water).state.c_str()) ) ;"
        - globals.set:
            id: total_water_pulses
            value:  "0"  

ota:
  password: "xxx"

wifi:
  ssid: !secret wifi_ssid
  password: !secret wifi_password

  # Enable fallback hotspot (captive portal) in case wifi connection fails
  ap:
    ssid: "${name} Fallback Hotspot"
    password: "xxx"

captive_portal:
    
# Enable Web server
web_server:
  port: 80
  
#  restart button  #
button:  
  - platform: restart
    name: "Restart device"
    
# Factory Reset Switch from ESPHome v2022.09    
  - platform: factory_reset
    name: Reset to factory settings   

text_sensor:
  - platform: version
    name: "ESPHome Version"

globals:
  - id: initial_water_usage
    type: float
    restore_value: yes      
    initial_value: '010' 

  - id: total_water_pulses
    type: int
    restore_value: yes
    
select:
  # water per m3 rate
  - platform: template
    name: "Pulserate water per m3"
    id: select_pulse_water
    optimistic: true
    options:
      - "0.0001"
      - "0.0005"
      - "0.001"  
      - "0.01"  
      - "0.1"                   
    initial_option: "0.001"
    restore_value: yes
    on_value:
      then:
        - logger.log:
            format: "Chosen option: %s "
            args: ["x.c_str()"]

binary_sensor:
  - platform: gpio
    id: water_sensor_status
    name: Water Proximity Sensor Status
    internal: False
    pin:
      number: D2
      inverted: true
      mode:
        input: true
    on_press:
      - then:
          lambda: id(total_water_pulses) += 1;  

  - platform: status
    name: "${friendly_name} Status"

sensor:
# watermeter pulsen
  - platform: pulse_meter #pulse_counter
    internal_filter_mode: "PULSE"
    internal_filter: 50ms  
    pin: D2
    id: watermeter_pulse_flow
    name: "Watermeter Flow"
    state_class: measurement
    unit_of_measurement: "l/min"    
    accuracy_decimals: 1
    icon: "mdi:water-pump"
    filters:
      lambda: return x * atof(id(select_pulse_water).state.c_str()) * 1000;

# Total watermeter     
    total:
      id: watermeter_pulse_meter_total
      name: "Watermeter Total"
      state_class: "total_increasing"
      device_class: water
      icon: mdi:water
      unit_of_measurement: "m³"
      accuracy_decimals: 3
      filters:
        - multiply: 0.001
      # filters:
      #  lambda: return x * atof(id(select_pulse_water).state.c_str());

      
# Watermeter stand bij benadering
  - platform: template
    id: watermeter_total
    name: "Watermeter Stand"
    state_class: "total_increasing"
    device_class: water
    icon: mdi:water
    unit_of_measurement: "m³"
    update_interval: 30s
    accuracy_decimals: 3
    lambda: return id(initial_water_usage) + (id(total_water_pulses) * atof(id(select_pulse_water).state.c_str()));

# Waterpulses  only web interface for debugging
  - platform: template
    id: watermeter_pulse_flow_total
    name: "Watermeter Flow Total"
    state_class: "total_increasing"
    accuracy_decimals: 0
    lambda: return id(total_water_pulses);
    internal: True

  - platform: wifi_signal
    name: "WiFi Signal"
    update_interval: 60s

  - platform: uptime
    name: "Uptime"
    icon: mdi:clock-outline
    update_interval: 60s
```

title: Multiple watermeter sensors
author: maciej871
body:
Hi,
I built your water meter and it's great! But I have a question, how do I add two such sensors to Home Assistant? I have two meters: one for hot water and one for cold water. I would like to measure water consumption from individual meters and total. How do I add a second sensor and how do I configure it?
Thanks for your help.

title: Working S0 kWh meters
author: huizebruin
body:
These meters have a standard **S0 pulse output** (open collector) and work with the S0tool:

| Brand        | Model                 | Compatibility | Notes                            |
|--------------|------------------------|----------------|----------------------------------|
| Eastron      | SDM120 / SDM220        | ✅ Compatible   | S0 pulse terminals               |
| ABB          | B23 / B24 series       | ✅ Compatible   | External S0 wiring required      |
| Schneider    | iEM3000 series         | ✅ Compatible   | Needs S0 connection setup        |
| Carlo Gavazzi| EM24 / EM340           | ✅ Compatible   | Pulse settings configurable      |
| Finder       | 7E.23 / 7E.36 series   | ✅ Compatible   | DIN rail mountable               |

title: S0tool 3.6.9 Update: Improved Flow & Energy Data Handling
author: huizebruin
body:
Date: March 8, 2026

We’re excited to announce the release of S0tool 3.6.9! This update focuses on improving data consistency and reliability for S0tool sensors, ensuring smoother integration with Home Assistant’s Energy Dashboard.

Key improvements include:

* Standardized flow unit: Flow readings are now consistently shown as L/min, limited to 2 decimal places.
* kWh meter filtering: Added a maximum filter to reduce spikes caused by irregular pulses.
* YAML standardization: File extensions updated from .yml to .yaml to follow common ESPHome and Home Assistant conventions.

This update enhances both accuracy and compatibility, making your energy and water monitoring more reliable than ever.

Tested on:

Water meters & S0 counters
